mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces corporate borrowing costs and compresses equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate crossing above 5.0 percent signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending and corporate earnings, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60 percent of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or out-of-sample accuracy drops below 52 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts confirm broad-based consumer demand expansion, which drives corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns in > 55 percent of cases, or interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the index of industrial production during a late-cycle expansion reflects weakening manufacturing output that precedes earnings downgrades and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in > 65 percent of declining production months, or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 18-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation combined with a rising federal funds rate indicates a tightening monetary policy stance that increases discount rates and compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL increases > 0.3 percent month-over-month while FEDFUNDS rises
failure_condition: The combined signal yields positive 20-day returns in > 60 percent of triggered months, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A consistent month-over-month increase in total nonfarm payrolls demonstrates sustained labor market strength that supports wage growth and consumer demand, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in > 58 percent of months with positive payroll growth, or out-of-sample directional accuracy falls below 51 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth decelerating below 2.0 percent annually signals demand exhaustion that reduces corporate profit margins and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annual growth rate falls below 2.0 percent
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 20-day returns in > 62 percent of quarters where GDP growth drops below the threshold, or predictive power vanishes in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial production and falling unemployment confirms a synchronized supply-side recovery that improves earnings expectations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The divergence signal fails to predict positive 5-day returns in > 60 percent of occurrences, or the joint effect loses explanatory power (adjusted R-squared < 0.05) in out-of-sample periods.
